name: DocuSign
description: An electronic signature and agreement workflow tool used for leases, approvals, vendor documents, and other high-friction document processes.
whyUseIt: In property operations, momentum often dies in document routing. DocuSign helps keep approvals, lease paperwork, variation letters, and vendor agreements moving without waiting on manual signatures and inbox confusion.
aiCapabilities: AI can extract key clauses, summarize agreement changes, and help flag where a document may need faster review or escalation.
workflowStage: Document execution, approvals, and agreement handoff
propertyManagerWorkflow: Managers use DocuSign to circulate lease-side documents, approvals for works, tenant notices, and contractor agreements while maintaining a clear execution trail.
managerExpects: Correct documents, correct recipients, tight turnaround, and no ambiguity over what version was signed.
aiWorkflowImpact: AI reduces review time by extracting critical dates or differences across versions before documents are sent for signature.
demoDescription: Prepare a simple approval package for a tenant variation and confirm the right signers, version notes, and follow-up checkpoints.

vocabulary.definition: The system record showing who received, opened, signed, and completed a document.
The package of documents, signers, and routing instructions sent for execution.
The other party signing or receiving the agreement or notice.

beginnerMistakes: Sending the wrong version of a document
Forgetting to confirm internal approvals before external routing
Treating execution as complete without saving the final record to the right file system
